coaching.title: Easy 5 Miles | Zone 2 — Build Phase
coaching.message: Phase 3, Week 10 of 22. 85 days to Julian Alps. Your calendar says easy 5 miles at Zone 2 — keep it conversational, HR under 155. You're in Antigua at ~1500m elevation, so effort will feel harder than flat sea-level pace; run by feel and heart rate, not by pace. Cobblestones on the main streets — head to the Alameda or out toward the hills for smoother footing and bonus vert practice. Morning temps are ideal. This is aerobic banking, not a workout — if it feels too easy, you're doing it right.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Pre-run (30–45 min before): 40–60g carbs — banana + toast or oats. Water + a small amount of sodium if you've been sweating. At 5 miles Zone 2, no mid-run fueling needed, but carry 500ml water given the altitude and Guatemala heat. Post-run within 30 minutes: 60–80g carbs + 20–30g protein — eggs, fruit, and a tortilla hit all the marks locally. Today's training load is moderate, so target ~280–300g total carbs for the day to support muscle glycogen recovery ahead of tomorrow.

coaching.title: Recovery Protocol
coaching.message: You already hit the gym this morning before the run — that's a meaningful combined load for a travel day. Post-run: 5 min easy walking cooldown, then 10 min hip flexor and calf mobility (cobbletones and altitude both tighten the posterior chain). Compression socks if you have them, especially if you're traveling or sitting long this afternoon. Aim for 7.5–8 hours tonight — altitude and travel fatigue compound. Sleep is the cheapest performance lever you have right now.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: 12 weeks out is where the race gets built or broken. The athletes who show up in Kranjska Gora strong aren't the ones who had perfect training — they're the ones who stayed consistent through the messy weeks. You're training across time zones, in Guatemala, squeezing miles between corp dev calls. Every session you complete in imperfect conditions is proof that you can handle the unpredictability of race day in the Julian Alps. This 5-miler counts. Log it.
